verses.1: For the music director; according to Shoshannim. A Psalm of David. Save me, God, For the waters have threatened my life.
verses.2: I have sunk in deep mud, and there is no foothold; I have come into deep waters, and a flood overflows me.
verses.3: I am weary with my crying; my throat is parched; My eyes fail while I wait for my God.
verses.4: Those who hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head; Those who would destroy me are powerful, those who oppose me with lies; What I did not steal, I then have to restore.
verses.5: God, You know my foolishness, And my guilt is not hidden from You.
verses.6: May those who wait for You not be ashamed because of me, Lord GOD of armies; May those who seek You not be dishonored because of me, God of Israel,
verses.7: Because for Your sake I have endured disgrace; Dishonor has covered my face.
verses.8: I have become estranged from my brothers, And a stranger to my mother’s sons.
verses.9: For zeal for Your house has consumed me, And the taunts of those who taunt You have fallen on me.
verses.10: When I wept in my soul with fasting, It became my disgrace.
verses.11: When I made sackcloth my clothing, I became a proverb to them.
verses.12: Those who sit in the gate talk about me, And songs of mockery by those habitually drunk are about me.
verses.13: But as for me, my prayer is to You, LORD, at an acceptable time; God, in the greatness of Your mercy, Answer me with Your saving truth.
verses.14: Rescue me from the mud and do not let me sink; May I be rescued from those who hate me, and from the depths of water.
verses.15: May the flood of water not overflow me Nor the deep swallow me up, Nor the pit close its mouth on me.
verses.16: Answer me, LORD, for Your mercy is good; According to the greatness of Your compassion, turn to me,
verses.17: And do not hide Your face from Your servant, For I am in distress; answer me quickly.
verses.18: Come near to my soul and redeem it; Ransom me because of my enemies!
verses.19: You know my disgrace, my shame, and my dishonor; All my enemies are known to You.
verses.20: Disgrace has broken my heart, and I am so sick. And I waited for sympathy, but there was none; And for comforters, but I found none.
verses.21: They also gave me a bitter herb in my food, And for my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
verses.22: May their table before them become a snare; And when they are at peace, may it become a trap.
verses.23: May their eyes grow dim so that they cannot see, And make their hips shake continually.
verses.24: Pour out Your indignation on them, And may Your burning anger overtake them.
verses.25: May their camp be desolated; May there be none living in their tents.
verses.26: For they have persecuted him whom You Yourself struck, And they tell of the pain of those whom You have wounded.
verses.27: Add guilt to their guilt, And may they not come into Your righteousness.
verses.28: May they be wiped out of the book of life, And may they not be recorded with the righteous.
verses.29: But I am afflicted and in pain; May Your salvation, God, set me safely on high.
verses.30: I will praise the name of God with song, And exalt Him with thanksgiving.
verses.31: And it will please the LORD better than an ox Or bull with horns and hoofs.
verses.32: The humble have seen it and are glad; You who seek God, let your heart revive.
verses.33: For the LORD hears the needy, And does not despise those of His who are prisoners.
verses.34: Heaven and earth shall praise Him, The seas and everything that moves in them.
verses.35: For God will save Zion and build the cities of Judah, So that they may live there and possess it.
verses.36: The descendants of His servants will inherit it, And those who love His name will live in it.
